brand.name: Boyum Law
brand.tagline: Your Family's Peace of Mind is Our Business

offer.what: Estate planning (wills, living trusts, special needs trusts, farm succession, asset protection) and probate administration, plus elder law, power of attorney, and Medicaid planning — all delivered by a single Nebraska-licensed attorney (Brad Boyum) in the Omaha metro
offer.coreOutcome: Families legally protect and transfer their assets to the next generation without losing wealth to probate, taxes, or administrative chaos
offer.pricePoint: Not publicly disclosed; typical Nebraska estate planning legal fees range $1,000–$5,000+ per engagement; one-off project fees, not subscription
offer.commitment: Project-based one-off engagements (estate plan or probate administration); probate cases can run 6–18 months
leadMagnets: Expert audio interviews / resources section on website

audience.icp: Omaha-metro Nebraska families, typically 45–70 years old, with accumulated assets (home, savings, farm, business) who have not yet formalized an estate plan, OR families navigating probate after the loss of a loved one
painPoints: Fear that hard-earned assets will be lost to probate costs or taxes
Confusion about which documents (will vs. trust vs. POA) are actually needed
Emotional overwhelm when dealing with a loved one's estate after death
Bank accounts frozen at death, creating immediate cash-flow crises for families
Uncertainty about whether an attorney is worth the cost vs. DIY online tools
Procrastination — 'we'll do it later' — until a health event forces the issue
segments: Pre-planning families: middle-aged adults who want to protect their estate proactively
Post-loss families: adult children or spouses navigating probate after a death
Farmers / agricultural landowners: need specialized succession planning
Elderly individuals: elder law, Medicaid planning, long-term care asset protection
Small business owners: business succession and asset protection via LLCs/trusts

salesCycle.length: Days to 2–3 weeks from first contact to retained client
salesCycle.trial: Free initial consultation — no cost, no obligation to retain
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Booked in-office consultation → personalized review of situation → engagement letter / retainer signed
salesCycle.topObjection: 'Do I really need a lawyer for this? Can I just use LegalZoom?' — cost and perceived complexity vs. DIY alternatives

ahaMoment: The free consultation itself: Brad walks the client through their specific situation and shows them exactly what documents they need and why — transforming a vague, scary topic into a clear, actionable plan. The moment a client understands their exposure (e.g., 'without a trust, your house goes through probate and costs your heirs X') is the activation event.

retention.recurring: Not subscription-based; repeat business comes from estate plan updates as life changes (marriage, divorce, new child, major asset purchase), or when a previously planned client needs probate services
retention.expansion: Upsell from simple will to full revocable living trust; add-on elder law / Medicaid planning as clients age; farm succession planning for landowners
retention.churnRisk: One-and-done clients who don't update documents for years; families who used a different attorney for estate planning then go elsewhere for probate; clients who postpone recommended updates until a crisis

lifecycleGaps: No visible welcome / onboarding email sequence after someone books or inquires
No lead-magnet nurture sequence to convert free-consultation no-shows or cold leads
No post-engagement re-engagement cadence (e.g., 'It's been 3 years — has your life changed? Time to review your estate plan')
No automated booking reminder / no-show recovery for consultation appointments
No referral or advocacy program to activate satisfied clients
No value newsletter to stay top-of-mind with past clients and referral partners (CPAs, financial advisors)
toneVoice: Professional yet warm and compassionate; family-first language; plain English over legal jargon; reassuring during emotionally difficult moments (death, aging, family conflict); honest about when an attorney is and isn't needed — builds trust through candor
compliance: Nebraska State Bar attorney advertising rules (no false or misleading claims, must include firm name/address); CAN-SPAM compliance required; no attorney-client privilege created by email; must avoid specific legal guarantees or outcome promises; GDPR unlikely to apply (local Nebraska focus)
